parsed_review.summary: CP00-442 is a descriptor-only RP14.P06 workflow-permission-slice pack (M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail x2 + M05 Permission And Audit Binding head x8 = 10 units, range RP14.P06.M04.S21-RP14.P06.M05.S08, risk A). Verified through Read/Grep/Glob only. Manifest, contract (current_pack, cp442_requirements, cp442_no_write_attestation, p06_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor, historical_pack_bindings tail), registry/service/validators source, model.test.js, and closeout-pack-plan.json are mutually consistent: unit_count 10, deliverable split test=2/security_audit=2/implementation=6, micro split M04=2/M05=8, handoff to CP00-443 at RP14.P06.M05.S09. The production_ready claim is appropriately conservative (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status implemented_pending_review); Hermes asserts only production_ready_candidate=true, gated behind human approval. All no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data flags are false across contract and source; a scan of packages/settlement/src found no fs/network/child_process/db/env operations (pure frozen-object builders). Claude stays read-only and non-final-approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, tools=Read/Grep/Glob). Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence/receipt (receipt_shape ...no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. CP178+ hardening forbidden-evidence list and a passing well-formed-JSON auth preflight are present. Downstream adjudication/construction-inspection artifacts are correctly absent at review time per the hardening sequence. No P0-P3 findings.
